text: The glass and steel around me evoke a sense of modernity and transparency, yet there is a clinical sterility that makes me feel detached, as though I am part of an experiment. The anticipation in the air—partly projected by the distant hum of technology, the seating arrangement, and the careful layout—elicits both curiosity and a mild sense of vigilance. I feel like an observer, yet aware that the very act of observing changes what is observed.
